expected_findings.summary: 2 1099-B lot(s) missing Code W on Form 8949 (disallowed loss $3,030)
expected_findings.detail: The broker reported a wash sale loss disallowed amount on these lots, but Form 8949 column (f) does not carry Code W and column (g) does not add the disallowance back. IRC §1091 requires the disallowed loss to be tracked and added to the replacement-lot basis. This is a mechanical compliance error that routinely triggers IRS CP2000 notices because the IRS wash-sale computer match sees the broker-reported amount and expects it on the return.
Form 8889 requires line 2 (contributions), line 6 (allowable), and line 13 (deduction) to reconcile with the TY2025 statutory limit. line 6 is $8,300 but TY2025 family limit is $8,550.
Foreign tax paid exceeds the election threshold for claiming the credit without Form 1116. Form 1116 is required and the credit may be limited.

A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Reese has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Reese is 38 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: Rowan has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Rowan is 38 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.

expected_findings.summary: AGI above Roth IRA phaseout — consider backdoor Roth
expected_findings.detail: AGI $285,400 is above the single Roth IRA phaseout ceiling ($165,000). Direct Roth contributions are not allowed. Consider the backdoor Roth strategy (nondeductible traditional IRA contribution + Roth conversion) if there is no pre-tax IRA balance to trigger pro-rata.
Reese has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
Rowan has contributed $6,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $17,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Compute AMT foreign tax credit separately on Form 1116
expected_findings.detail: At high income with foreign tax paid, AMT requires a parallel Form 1116 using AMT sourcing rules under §59(a). The regular FTC and AMT FTC are often different.

expected_findings.summary: Underpayment: need $35,475 withheld for 110% safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: Prior year AGI exceeded $150,000, so the §6654 safe harbor requires 110% of prior year total tax ($35,475) to be paid via withholding or quarterly estimates. Currently $33,700 is withheld; consider a Q4 estimated payment or increased withholding of $1,775 to avoid the §6654 penalty.

expected_findings.summary: Need $46,235 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.

Based on a shortfall of $1,775 and the TY2025 applicable interest rate of ~8%, the underpayment penalty is approximately $71. Consider the annualized income method (Schedule AI) if income was lumpy across quarters.
